hero.kicker: currently · Lead AI Engineer @ Stealth Startup
headline: Alonso
Peralta
hero.lead: AI software engineer building tools that turn two-week jobs into ten-minute ones. Lead AI Engineer at @@Stealth Startup@@. M.S. Computer Science at Texas A&M.

paragraphs: I was born in Peru and lived there for sixteen years. Then Alaska for two years to finish high school. After that, Texas A&M for a B.S. in Computer Engineering, and now an M.S. in Computer Science starting this fall.

AI got me because of impact. Pick the right tool and a two-to-three week document workflow drops to ten minutes. I have watched it happen. The part of this field I keep coming back to is work that moves real numbers for real people.

Right now I lead AI engineering at @@Stealth Startup@@ remotely while finishing my masters. Before this I did a software engineering study abroad in Singapore, built a 3D-printer-farm platform in Belgium with @@OpenHub@@, worked on drug-discovery RAG for a biotech sponsor through @@Aggies Create@@, and went through the @@Headstarter@@ fellowship with mentors from Google, Meta, and Apple.
Off the clock: billiards, traveling, videogames, reading about finance, startups, and quantum news. Bilingual in English and Spanish.

now.lead: What I'm focused on this week, and what's coming up. Updated when something changes.

entries.body: Graduation week. Wrapping the B.S. and starting the Lead AI Engineer role at the stealth startup.
Settling into the Lead role. Drafting the AI roadmap and figuring out the next services. Some prep on the side for the M.S.
M.S. Computer Science begins at Texas A&M. Staying at the startup through it.
Billiards, lifting, videogames, reading about startups, finance, and quantum news. Spanish stays sharp.

work.lead: A few things worth pointing at. Most of what I build sits behind APIs and inside infra; these are the ones I can talk about.

items.title: alonsoperalta.com
items.blurb: This site. Next.js 15 with React 19, statically exported and served on Firebase Hosting. GitHub Actions pipeline lints, builds, and deploys on every push to main. Animated orbit-of-interests constellation on the home page. Single accent color, oversized grotesque headlines, custom SVG illustrations for every project.

items.title: SDS Caller
items.blurb: AI voice agent for chemical companies. Sounds human, and takes input by reading emotional intent and situational context. RAG over Safety Datasheets. 97% retrieval accuracy on all queries by chemical trade name and scenario.

items.title: ExtractAI
items.blurb: Document data extraction service. Cut $2,500 per 1,000 pages to $35. Brought processing from two or three weeks down to ten minutes. 98% OCR accuracy on Safety Datasheets. Built and shipped on GCP.

items.title: T.R.A.C.K.
items.blurb: Senior design (CSCE 483) for Texas A&M Formula SAE Electric. On-car telemetry display plus a pit-side web app for live monitoring and configuration. Built the React frontend, contributed to the Express/Firebase backend, and owned the cloud system design. Firebase auth, WebSocket streaming, Fly.io.

items.title: Wavelet for Hearts
items.blurb: Co-authored research paper for MATH 414 (Fourier Wavelets) at Texas A&M. Wavelet-based singularity detection in ECG signals across five wavelets and two pipelines (CWT and DWT). Best F1 0.907 on QRS detection against the MIT-BIH Arrhythmia Database.

items.title: LLMRouter
items.blurb: Personal project for CSCE 642 (Deep Reinforcement Learning) at Texas A&M. DRL system that picks the right LLM per prompt, like the 'auto' model selector in coding agents, balancing cost and capability per task. Five agents (DQN, PPO, LinUCB, PickLLM, Greedy) trained on RouterBench, with feature-based and embedding-based variants and a Streamlit evaluation dashboard.

items.title: Practice Problem Generator
items.blurb: CSCE 431 (Software Engineering) project from my Singapore study abroad, built for the Texas A&M Engineering Department. Ruby on Rails platform with 40 templates across 10 topics, generating unlimited practice variants. Team of 6, faculty client.

items.title: MedGet
items.blurb: Drug-candidate research platform for biotech sponsor NeuroX1. Hybrid retrieval and RAG through LangChain (LLM and BioMixtral 8x22B). 5x faster data access and 62% lower cost per employee hour. The sponsor took it on after delivery.

items.title: PrinterHub
items.blurb: Sole developer on a full-stack platform to remotely manage a 3D-printer farm in Belgium. Took the team from a handful of printers running in parallel to seven times that. React, Node, Python, Firebase.

items.title: Headstarter Fellowship
items.blurb: Four full-stack AI projects in three months. LangChain, Pinecone, AWS, GCP, computer vision. Mentored by engineers from Google, Meta, Apple, and DoorDash, plus founders and researchers from Stanford and Berkeley.

repo.label: Inventory Tracker, Customer Support, BrainstormAI, FlashcardAI

roles.title: Lead AI Engineer
roles.blurb: Just promoted. Leading and designing multiple AI services. Responsible for the product AI roadmap.

roles.title: AI Software Engineer
roles.blurb: Built and shipped ExtractAI, an OCR pipeline on GCP. 98% accuracy on safety datasheets. Replaced weeks of manual document processing with minutes.

roles.title: AI Solutions Specialist
roles.blurb: Internal R&D on AI solutions for company clients. Prototyped, scoped, and figured out what could become real services.

roles.title: Co-Lead Full-Stack Developer
roles.blurb: Co-led a team of 4 to deliver MedGet for biotech sponsor NeuroX1. Hybrid retrieval and RAG through LangChain (LLM and BioMixtral 8x22B). 5x faster access and 62% lower cost per employee hour. The sponsor took it on after delivery.

roles.title: Full-Stack Developer
roles.blurb: Prototyped the first MedGet with Streamlit and early RAG. Delivered the MVP that secured $2,000 per month in sponsorship.

roles.title: Software Engineering Fellow
roles.blurb: Four full-stack AI projects in three months. Mentors from Google, Meta, Apple, and DoorDash. Founders and researchers from Stanford and Berkeley.

roles.title: Software Engineering Intern
roles.blurb: Belgium. Sole developer on PrinterHub, a full-stack platform to remotely manage a 3D-printer farm. 7x more printers in parallel.

education.title: B.S. Computer Engineering · Math Minor
education.blurb: GPA 3.65 / 4.00. Coursework: software engineering, databases, algorithms, deep RL, AI, computer systems design.

abroad.title: Software Engineering Abroad
abroad.blurb: Texas A&M study abroad in Belgium. Led directly into the OpenHub internship in Louvain-la-Neuve.

stack.value: JavaScript · TypeScript · Python · C++ · SQL · Ruby · Cypher
LangChain · LangGraph · Pinecone · RAG · OCR · Agents

contact.lead: Email is the way. I read everything and usually reply within a few days.

nodes.label: AI
nodes.description: Where most of my attention goes. Pulled in by what AI can do once it stops being a demo and starts moving real numbers.

nodes.label: Agents
nodes.description: Tool-use loops and voice agents. Structured traces, retries that don't pretend a failed call succeeded. The part of an agent that isn't the model.

nodes.label: Retrieval
nodes.description: Hybrid retrieval, RAG, reranking. Built it for drug discovery, document extraction, and chemical-emergency lookup. Most of the win is in the data shape.

nodes.label: Systems
nodes.description: GCP, AWS, Terraform, CI/CD. The unsexy work that lets the cool work ship. Also the systems layer underneath: distributed services, databases, the foundations that decide whether things wake you up at 3 AM.

nodes.label: Full-Stack
nodes.description: React, Node, Python, Firebase. Years of putting buttons on top of databases until they actually do something useful. Belgium, NeuroX1, and A&M classes all built this muscle.

nodes.label: Research
nodes.description: More R&D than academic, but the wavelet-paper side counts too. As AI Solutions Specialist at the stealth startup I spent months prototyping AI ideas and scoping what could ship. The M.S. coming up is applied AI focused, not pure research.

nodes.label: Startups
nodes.description: Working at one in stealth right now, and built for one (NeuroX1) earlier. I read about how startups get funded and structured more than I should. The shape of how things actually get built fascinates me.

nodes.label: ML · DL · RL
nodes.description: Where the curiosity started. PyTorch, scikit-learn, Gymnasium, deep RL coursework. A quiet appreciation for systems that converge.

nodes.label: Finance
nodes.description: Capital structures, markets, the way money moves. Adjacent to the startup curiosity. Mostly in the reading column for now.

nodes.label: Quantum
nodes.description: Highly fascinated by where it's going. The hardware is interesting; the software side, particularly its intersection with AI and ML, is where I think the long-term impact lands. Little hands-on yet. Building it in via personal projects alongside the M.S.
